items.title: Oil Climbs Above $90 as U.S.-Iran Conflict Deepens, Raising Global Supply Concerns
items.desc: Renewed U.S.-Iran military escalation and mounting concerns over the Strait of Hormuz are driving oil prices higher while raising fresh questions about inflation, shipping security, and global economic stability.

items.title: Iran Claims Strikes on U.S. Bases in Kuwait and Jordan as Fighting Intensifies Across Gulf
items.desc: Tehran says it hit American military assets, while U.S. strikes kill at least one near Tabriz amid fresh security incidents in the Strait of Hormuz and Bahrain

items.title: Trump Says U.S. Hitting Iran 'Very Hard' to Honor Fallen American Troops
items.desc: The U.S. President Donald Trump says renewed U.S. strikes honor fallen service members as the Iran conflict escalates, with mounting casualties and growing regional instability.

items.title: CENTCOM Mourns Fallen Troops in Kurdistan Region and Jordan

items.title: Turkiye's grain exports to Iraq hit $702M in H1
items.desc: Shafaq News- Ankara Iraq ranked as the largest importer of grains, pulses, oilseeds, and related products from Turkiye in the first half of this year, purchasing goods worth $701.95 million, Turkiye’s Food Exporters said on Sunday. items.title: Ninth night of US strikes draws Iranian counterstrikes on Gulf bases
items.desc: Shafaq News- Tehran/ Washington (Updated at 9:48) The United States carried out a new wave of strikes on Iran early Monday, the ninth consecutive night of its air campaign, according to the US Central Command (CENTCOM). items.title: Iraq detains 35 officials in weekly corruption sweep
items.desc: Shafaq News- Baghdad Iraqi authorities detained at least 35 officials and employees in six separate corruption cases between July 12 and July 19, according to a Shafaq News review. The detentions spanned five provinces or operational jurisdictions and seven government sectors, from the Defense Ministry to customs posts on the Kurdistan Region's boundary. items.title: Oil surges above $90 on Gulf escalation
items.desc: Shafaq News Brent oil prices rose 2% to more than $90 per barrel on Monday, ‌as escalating U.S.-Iran hostilities in the Middle East restricted oil shipments through the Strait of Hormuz. Brent crude futures climbed $2.09, or 2.37%, to $90.19 by 0241 GMT, touching the highest since June 11, extending gains after rising 15.9% last week, its biggest weekly gain since ​April. items.title: World Cup 2026 awards: Spain sweep top individual prizes
items.desc: Shafaq News- New York/ New Jersey Spain capped their World Cup 2026 title by winning three of the tournament’s principal awards, with Rodri receiving the Golden Ball, Unai Simon the Golden Glove, and Pau Cubarsi the Young Player Award. items.title: JD Vance announces birth of baby boy with wife Usha

items.title: In pictures: Joyous fans gather across the US and Canada to watch World Cup final

items.title: Who is Andy Burnham, the U.K.'s new prime minister?
items.desc: On Monday, former Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham succeeds Keir Starmer as the United Kingdom's prime minister. Burnham's politics have been shaped by England's north-south class divide.

items.title: An Israeli paraglider tries to save migrating swifts that nest in the Western Wall
items.desc: Swifts have nested in Jerusalem's Western Wall since biblical times. Now one Israeli paraglider is trying to save them.

items.title: Deadly overnight Russian attack on Ukraine targets the capital
items.desc: Russia launched 41 missiles and 125 attack drones across Ukraine overnight, according to the Ukrainian air force.

items.title: Cuban artist and dissident exiled from country after 5 years in prison arrives in US
items.desc: A famous Cuban dissident artist and musician, Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara, arrived in Miami on Saturday after being released from a five-year prison sentence on the condition that he leave his country.

items.title: Andrew and Tristan Tate arrested in Florida on charges of rape and sex trafficking
items.desc: Influencer Andrew Tate and his brother Tristan have been arrested in Miami after British authorities issued 38 new charges against them, and said they are "seeking extradition" for the two men.
